title: network access broken on rootful podman fedora 42 kinoite, bootc variant
body:
### Issue Description

All network access is broken for me, DNS is just the first thing that fails inside container

$ sudo curl -I example.com && sudo podman run --rm -it quay.io/fedora/fedora-kinoite:42 curl -I example.com
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/html
ETag: "84238dfc8092e5d9c0dac8ef93371a07:1736799080.121134"
Last-Modified: Mon, 13 Jan 2025 20:11:20 GMT
Cache-Control: max-age=771
Date: Wed, 13 Aug 2025 10:57:42 GMT
Connection: keep-alive

curl: (6) Could not resolve host: example.com

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. sudo curl -I example.com && sudo podman run --rm -it quay.io/fedora/fedora-kinoite:42 curl -I example.com
2.
3.


### Describe the results you received

Network broken

### Describe the results you expected

Network working

comments.body: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25072 seems like the same issue, --network host works

https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25072 is using WSL which uses its own network stack which may introduce its own problems so I doubt that would be the same problem.

What does network access broken mean exactly can you capture packages and see where they are getting dropped?
For rootful networking you need for check the firewall rules, bridge/veth interfaces and make sure the ip_forwarding sysctl is turned on (that should be done automatically but maybe something unset that.)

You seem to be using older versions, upstream only supports the latest version and those should already be shipped in fedora for a while. And I assume to fails for all image snot just quay.io/fedora/fedora-kinoite:42 right?

Seems to be on:

```
$ sudo sysctl net.ipv4.ip_forward
net.ipv4.ip_forward = 1
```

Update podman, same result:

If it's related to docker, I want both docker and podman to coexist on this machine

Docker block our traffic by default, you must tell docker to allow other traffic
https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Changes/NetavarkNftablesDefault#Known_Issue_with_docker

Another hack for my Containerfile I guess:

https://github.com/ericcurtin/staging/commit/cf64b4e4d8e793a909ef3e9b2197c9f554570253
